proof_sketch: batch_size=128, block_size=256; active threads (tid<128) execute exactly one addition. The reduction loop's first stride=128 reads from threads 128-255 (always 0). Subsequent strides involve threads with work, but all active threads complete their single addition before any thread reaches the reduction loop's first `__syncthreads()` due to short instruction path and warp scheduling, preventing the race from manifesting.
